Title: review
Review: Exactly the same remote as the original. Good price compare to others remotes. There no need of learning how to used it since it  has the same characteristics of my old remote.

Title: Great hiking boots
Review: These are the most comfortable boots I own. I am an avid hiker/ backpacker and these boots have never let me down. They provide great support, especially as I have ankle issues, and they are solid and sturdy enough to.handle any terrain. Very light, and breathable, while still being essentially waterproof (I wouldn't submerge them, but morning dew, wet grass, puddles, etc are handled with ease and keep my feet nice and dry). These feel like a comfortable pair of tennis shoes even after miles and miles. There are more expensive boots our there, but the just aren't worth the extra money. These will do for anyone e but the most extreme outdoor type.

Title: Good way to tint and color brows
Review: I have sparse eyebrows which have a lot of gray. I tend to get them tinted at least once a month and really should do it more often. When I saw this I was thrilled to try it to help maintain my brows between tinting. On the plus side, the lighter brown color works well for me and it lasts a long time. I am not thrilled with the applicator which makes applying difficult (I wish it was more of a brush than a pointed applicator). I can recommend this to anyone looking to darken or enhance their sparse eye brows.

Title: Not cotton- not comfortable- not happy!
Review: These fit like hospital undies. They are not cute on- they fit tight on waist and around my thigh- and I’m a small girl and got a m when I normally wear sz s. They are see through and HATE the fabric. Not breathable, not cotton. Also, in the picture they look cute and they just really are not cute at all. Not happy with these- save your money!

Title: Great innovation, but not very durable
Review: Very interesting design, but after 5 uses, one of the tips broke. Still usable, but doesn't look good.

Title: Fits but colour is not as pictured
Review: It does fit but the colour is completely different from the picture here it is a lot more fuschia and almost neon colour like orange even not flattering at all. Not wearing this probably ever, maybe just the top, colour is scary as hell. I guess if you don't mind the colour being different then it's fine, on another note the fit is good though and easy to wear so no problem there just extremely flashy.

Title: Better purchase than what you will get at sephora
Review: The product fits nicely into my eyebrow routine. I like that it does not turn white like other eye jells do. I find that it holds the hair in the direction that the hair is growing only. I do dye my eye brows which makes the hair thicker and coarse, maybe that is why. I think it would hold natural eyebrows up well that have not been tinted

Title: Garbage. Review: Don't even bother with these unless you're ONLY using them for a lens protector.
That's about all they do.

As for the description:

"CPL: Polarizers let you acheive vibrant color saturation, reduce or eliminate glare from non-metallic object.
F-DL: Fluorescent light correction filter gives true-to-life color rendition. Fluorescent filter dramatically increases color saturation"

They don't do any of that. They actually ruined my photos rather than enhancing them.

Go buy a REAL filter from B&W. I got one there after ordering these and they are 10,000% better, but also more pricey.

My guess is these cheap ones were built in China/India from scrap class/plastic while B&W uses high quality metals and glasses for their filters.

Anyway that's all I have to say, don't be disappointed and just skip these altogether.

Title: Nice tunic top, runs a little small
Review: Super cute tunic top. Great length (goes over my leggings perfect). I am deducting a star because I found around the waist the shirt fit me a little too small (muffin top was showing a bit). I wish I had bought a size larger. But, all in all, well made top pretty red color.

Title: lovely
Review: I received  an authentic  doc martin box and boots. These will require breaking in for a couple weeks prior to wearing them out side. These boots will last many years to come.

Title: Bought as a gift, then bought myself one!
Review: We have Apple products in our home, but none of us had Apple Watches.  I bought this for my college daughter who didn't ever really wear a watch.  She was so surprised and loves it and never takes it off!  And again, Apple didn't disappoint...set up was easy and usage is very intuitive.  I bought the black one for my husband for Christmas too, simply for the fall detection (seizures) and even at 60+, he found it easy to use.  They were so in love that the day after Christmas, I purchased one too and am really enjoying it.  You really can't go wrong when it comes to Apple.

Review: Not at all what I thought it was

Title: Good quality running socks
Review: These are nice running socks. They are a little higher than I typically use, but outside of that they seem well made. Absorbs sweat well, helps to cushion the run. Have washed them at least twice already and so far so good when it comes to holding up to washing and I see no fading in the color. They are also reasonably priced. These would make a good stocking stuffer for the runner in your life during the Christmas season.

Title: Great Blender!
Review: This is great- just make sure you wash it regularly or it will become a bacteria breeding ground. Just place in a bowl with hot water and soap and place in the microwave for a minute or 2. That will kill bacteria and then rinse it out and let it dry. It’s a great tool for blending foundation. Especially the tip around your nose.

It has a large face, but the actual watch is small.  If you can't see the markings on the watch, you can't tel what any of the gages are telling you.

Rather than moisturizing, it actually seemed to dry my lips. It was too late to return by the time I made up my mind about the product, and I actually threw it in the trash.

Title: Moisturizes nicely but leaves a pea-soup yellowish green film behind Review: The moisturizing ability of this face cream is very good. I applied a small amount all over my clean dry face and neck (avoiding the eyes), and my skin soaked it up. It also left my face and neck feeling "plump" and smooth. However, the cream is green and when I use a tissue to wipe my face, I noticed a yellow-greenish tinge on the tissue. This could also get on clothes, etc. This is like having make-up applied to the face. If I wanted something that wears off onto my clothes, I would have applied make-up. I hate products whose color wears off especially when I am not wearing any make-up. 

In summary, despite its good moisturizing ability, I do not like the smell of this product or the color which transfers onto clothes, tissues, etc.

Title: Failed us
Review: Purchased this in September, 2016 from Amazon. Put in bedroom for occasional use, typically when family visited to entertain their kids.  It was used possibly twice but when tried to use it on 12/27/16 the player would not respond to any commands:  tray wouldn't open and home page would not come up.  Unfortunately, the window to return it closed in October.  Disappointed with this particular Blu-Ray player since we have had a similar one in the living room which has never failed us.  Lucky with one player and unlucky with this one.

Title: Eats batteries
Review: Overall, this radio delivers on what it promises, but it goes through batteries quickly.  I also found the manual charging to be less-than-great with a lot of work producing little time.  Great for burning calories but not what I want in an emergency.  I also wish that the storm / emergency on would turn the unit off after 5 minutes to prevent battery drain when no one is home and the unit was left in standby mode.

Title: Three or five stars. Depends on how serious you are Review: It is, by far, the best GPS I have ever had. Big, bright, clear display; intuitive menus; useful information. So what's wrong. The Smart Link app that provides the unit with weather and other information. The connection is Bluetooth, but it often fails to relink after you re-approach the unit. And getting it relinked is sometimes painfully difficult. It will show the units connected on one page and not connected on another. It can be damn frustrating. There is also a feature that shows where you parked your car. I used it the other day, and it reported my car was 32 miles away. The weather is M-Powered. Problem is the M-Power iPhone app itself is not highly rated. One problem is that it will display a warning or watch area, but will sometimes fail to delete that map layer so that you can see the actual storms...which you can see with your eye and on the unit sometime later. Garmin needs to do some serious work on this feature.

If you are serious about what the Smart Link feature can do, do not buy this unit until Garmin gets serious about fixing it.

Title: Needed these!
Review: This is a handy set of tools. It has all of the small screw driver bits that you need for opening the battery part on toys or just for craft making. It comes in a very sturdy carrying/storage case that is made of a nice thick plastic that shuts closed tightly. I as a woman have been looking for a set like this for the reasons listed above.

Title: Useful pedicure stand as long as it is not on its highest two settings Review: For the current price of $18.99 one gets a pedicure stand and one toe spacer. I personally could do without the spacer.

The stand is adjustable to several levels. However, it is narrow and because of that, it is unsteady when it is on its highest two levels. This works for me when it is only slightly inclined and for that reason I like it. However, if this were about four inches wider, I suspect it would not be so unsteady. This can also be used for those who need to elevate their feet to put on their shoes; however, it really needs to be wider.

If this were four inches wider, then not only would this be a pedicure foot rest but it would also be an assistive device for elevating the feet to put on shoes. It would have received 5 stars from me had this been the case. In its current state, this is good as long as one is not using all the levels.

Title: Thin hair towel and thin exfoliating glove; functional but not high quality - 2.5 stars Review: I am not impressed by this towel and glove. These items are functional but they are not the best quality.

The towel is large and thin. It has a button and small cloth loop at the tail that allows me to twist the turban and then secure the excess to the back button. However, since this is large, there is a little play in the turban and it is not as tight as I would like. It is absorbent, however.

The exfoliating glove is scratchy, or a bit rough, and very thin. This also works but it is not high quality.

Overall, I am not impressed by this set. They are functional and work as designed, so in that sense they are okay. I personally consider [[ASIN:B09VL1JQGF these]] Poday towels exceptional compared to this--of course, those do not come with an exfoliating glove but then one gets two towels for almost the same price minus the glove. The current price of $9.99 is insane for this.

Title: Works. Super ugly, though.

They do work well with my Sony noise-canceling headphones at work, but they do not look pleasant with my work setup. You have to assemble the base by placing the holder and the base into the pole, and that's pretty much it. I wouldn't choose these again if I had the choice, though.

Title: This protected me for about 6 hours Review: I usually use nothing but Old Spice Swagger solid with the active ingredient of aluminum zirconium tetrachlorohydrex gly 17%. This Dove solid has no aluminum and only plant-based ingredients. Usually, products like these only last me about 2-3 hours; however, the protection lasted about 6 hours for me and definitely not the 24-hours specified.

I take a shower every 12 hours because I sweat a lot, so I can never attest to 24-hour protection. This is Eucalyptus scented but I have to put my nose to the stick to detect any scent and it is only slight--neither offensive or great to my senses.

This works better than other non-aluminum deodorants I tried previously. The current price of $24.14 ($28.40 minus 15%) comes to an expensive $8.05 per 2.6 ounce solid stick as this is a 3-pack.

Eh.   Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. All of my reviews reflect my honest, personal experience with the reviewed item - your experience may be different. I am not influenced by any outside source. I receive/accept NO free products or discounts that are not available to all shoppers- ever. For some reason our shopper ranks are no longer visible - so, to give you a little more info about me, I am a top 50 reviewer (#30 the highest rank achieved). Those numbers used to fluctuate over time - up and down but I noticed that they stopped updating regularly - perhaps to phase them out. It's a shame because it did help you see who has been around the longest and who is a trustworthy reviewer.  I've been doing reviews for over 25 years with Amazon - over 6,000 reviews posted, those reviews have been viewed well over 50,000 times, including over 24,000 likes. Bottom line, I pay for all my stuff, just like you do.

Title: Not my kind of facial therapy but I really did like the refreshing results Review: There is one mask in this pouch sandwiched between two protective sheets that have to be removed. Once removed, the mask is a gel-like sloppily-wet substance. I wish I had another person to put this on me. Nevertheless, I was able to flatten it out and set it on my face. I waited the requisite 20 minutes before removing it and then let the remaining solution soak in as per the directions.

The results were wonderful in the sense that my face felt refreshed and soft. However, I absolutely hate having to go through this. First, there is an ick factor to me with regard to the mask due to its slimy feel. Second, I am not a person who relaxes well; having to lay down and do nothing for 20 minutes other than relax is almost torture to me. Obviously, this is not my kind of therapy. I am glad I tried it but it is not something I want to repeat even though I am rewarded with a rejuvenated face.

Title: Attractive set of 4 Haitian soaps; honey of Moringa is the last ingredient in every bar - 3.5 stars Review: I never heard of Moringa oil nor have I ever had a product from Haiti. This soap's last ingredient is "honey of Moringa," which I assume is Moringa oil.

There are four 100-gram (3.527 ounce) bars and all but one is green in color. The citrus, lavender, and leaf powder are all green and the baby roots powder is white. I like the fragrance of the citrus and lavender bars but not so much the other two. The etched design on the bars is very nice and is most likely an etching of the Moringa tree.

The soap lathers and cleans well. I must admit that I was very confused by the Amazon title and unsure if this is a face soap, hand soap, or skin soap since it had everything in the title. I believe this is a face soap that can be used as all-purpose soap.

The current price of $23.75 comes to $5.94 per small bar. I give this set 3.5 stars rounded down to 3 stars.

I have to assume it's leather (as the ad says it is) but I would never be purchasing this if I knew what it was like. The inside is constructed well and there are places to put credit cards along with your passport. As of this writing there is no price listed for this passport holder (says currently unavailable). If it's cheap enough, it might be worthwhile getting. Otherwise, shop around a bit.

The material is thin and a bit rough. It's not soft at all. The boat neck line is interesting, but no matter how I position the neck area, my bra straps are always showing. The stripes look nice. It washes fine. Overall, I just find it to be okay.

Title: Protective hard case with very minimal padding to fit Sigma 16mm e-mount lens
Review: Bought this for a Sigma 16mm f/1.4 Sony e-mount lens with hood. Easily fits front down with lens hood reversed.. but also closes (with a little persuasion with hood attached (lens facing up). As noted in previous reviews... it is a case... but NOT a padded case. I solved this by cutting and adding the heavy foam that comes in my B+W filters both above and below to protect lens from minor bumps. I feel safe placing a $450 lens in this case. Only down side is the outside is slippery and almost shot out of my hands... add a few rubber bands to solve this. There is also a tiny loop and mini clip... would have preferred some larger loop to enable attaching options. plenty of room on sides of lens to carry a microfiber cleaning cloth.  With any bottom padding I would have rated a 4 star

Title: One Star
Review: Waste of money

Title: Nice rich moisturizer
Review: This is a rich moisturizing cream which comes in a small (1.69 oz jar). It goes on lightly, smooths easily and is absorbed quickly into my skin. I do like the fragrance, somewhat fruity, but I would honestly prefer it in a light orangy scent (simply because of the vitamin C content). I am deducting a star as I have used over the counter products (like Oil of Olay) that works just as well at less than 1/2 the cost. This is my first time using an M. Asam product and I was very impressed. My skin does feel softer and smoother after using for almost 3 weeks now. I do recommend the product, just wish it was in the $20 range.

Title: don't waste your money
Review: very poor quality… as I try to wipe the excess fluid off and it just ripped. I put on the second piece, wiped very gently, check it the next morning, the edge has shriveled. It dried and curl up at the edge!!

Title: Great product. Worth the money
Review: Needed a book light that was east to easy and as dim as possible that still works. This is great! Cheap and worth the price!

Title: One of the best handsoaps in the market today!
Review: This Wavertree & London Natural Liquid Soap is wonderful. It's made from pure, natural sustainable plant oils and extracts, smells great and is great as a hand soap. It contains shea butter, so it helps to keep my hands from getting overly dry from repeated washings. I keep this on my kitchen counter next to the sink and have been using it exclusively over the past month. I can highly recommend.

Title: Tight fit at the ankle Review: I haven't worn them anyplace, just tried them on. I wear an 8- 8 1/2 for width. In a boot I wear a 9 for extra ease..... I purchased a size 10, thinking they could be slipped on quickly...while running out the door. Wrong--they are very snug at the ankle... I don't have full ankles or calves.
The boot is heavier than I expected them to be. The reason I eventually bought them was the 50% price reduction.

Title: Bluetooth Headset with Mic, Water Resistant, Stereo, 8 Hour, Noise Reduction Ear Tips by IPXOZO Review: Disappointed. Everything about them is a struggle. Charging. Syncing Bluetooth. Tinny sound. Controls. I would take a pass if offered these again.
lifetime warranty for IPXOZO wireless earbuds.
What’s in the Box?
Bluetooth Headphones
Portable Carrying Case
Charging Cable
Ear Tips (2xSmall, 2xMedium, 2xLarge)
Specifications:
Bluetooth: 4.1+EDR
Transmission Distance: 33ft (11 yards)
Battery: Lithium-ion Polymer 110mAh
